rows.notes: Top-of-page title clearly written across the page with no amounts in the right columns. Very legible script and separated from the tabulated area.

rows.description: Q Die Nov:r 1783, ad eundem Diem 1784,
rows.notes: Date line directly under title; no monetary figures in the rightmost columns. Handwriting clear enough to read the Latin date phrase.
Section heading (arrearage receipts). No amounts. Text is clear and centered above the list of entries.
Large clear '63' in the pounds column and '3' in the shillings column. Pence column appears blank. Vertical ruling alignment consistent with other rows.
Three numerals visible aligned in three right columns: '14' (pounds), '2' (shillings), '1' (pence). All digits legible and within valid ranges.
Pounds and shillings ('7' and '1') are clear. The pence digit is small and slightly faded; it reads like a '1' but could be faint — chosen '1' based on alignment with the pence column.
Pounds and shillings ('7' and '1') are clear. The pence figure is faint and similar to the previous row; read as '1' by column alignment but somewhat ambiguous.
Right columns show '6' (pounds), '11' (shillings) and a clear '8' in the pence column. All digits well aligned and within valid ranges.
Pounds '5' and shillings '10' clearly visible. Pence column appears blank or omitted; no pence digit legible.
A single '2' is visible aligned under the pounds column; no shillings or pence are written. Alignment and digit are clear.
Three numerals visible: '2' (pounds), '2' (shillings), '6' (pence). Clear alignment with the three vertical ruling positions.
Shows '1' under pounds and '10' under shillings. Pence column blank. Words before amounts are slightly abbreviated but legible.
Pounds '10' and shillings '13' are clear and properly aligned; no pence figure present.

rows.description: Do Comyns & Donathan, obin Harforth, pro Bien.
rows.notes: Pounds '13' and shillings '6' clearly visible at right. Text before amounts a little compressed but readable; no pence digit.

Right-side numerals read as '3' (pounds), '6' (shillings), '8' (pence). The pence digit is slightly smaller but aligns with the pence column; spelled name 'Greenhow' read from faint letters and may vary slightly.
Shows '13' in pounds column and '6' in shillings column. Pence column blank. Handwriting reasonably clear.

There is a very small solitary '1' near the pence column for this row; pounds and shillings appear blank/dashed. The single digit is faint and could be a stray mark; recorded here as 1d because alignment suggests the pence column, but confidence is low.
A clear '3' is visible under the pounds column. No shilling or pence digits present.
Clear '10' under pounds column. No shilling or pence figures. Handwriting crisp for this row.
Three digits visible: '3' pounds, '19' shillings and '1' pence. 19 shillings is valid (<20). The middle number (19) is slightly crowded but legible.
Clear '5' (pounds), '1' (shillings), '7' (pence). Alignment and digits are distinct and valid.

rows.description: W. Taylor & Wife, pro An. & Vimid.
rows.notes: Digits '8', '15', '9' clearly seen across the three columns. Handwriting neat; values within valid ranges.
Rightmost columns show '8' (pounds), '12' (shillings) and '11' (pence). All numerals clear and conform to currency ranges.

Numerals read as '1' (pounds), '10' (shillings), '8' (pence). After the pence digit there is a small looped/forked mark consistent with a three‑farthings sign (3q = 0.75) in these ledgers; the fraction reading is somewhat smeared, so confidence moderate.
Shows '2' in pounds and '9' in shillings. Pence column blank. The text label is similar to row 23 but indicates 'pro Trien' (spelling slightly abbreviated); digits are clear.

Digits read as '3' (pounds), '5' (shillings), '2' (pence). After the pence there is a small loop resembling the half‑penny 'ob' mark; interpreted as 0.5. The fraction mark is smudged but aligns with typical halfpenny notation in this document.
'1' in pounds and '6' in shillings are clear. No pence figure. Handwriting crisp for these numerals.
Single clear '1' under pounds column; no shillings or pence recorded. Description legible.

Small values align with the two rightmost columns: pounds column blank, shillings '9', pence '6'. The pounds column being blank indicates amount less than one pound; digits are readable though somewhat small.
